message:
feat(alloc,core): add allocation tracking infrastructure

ffs-alloc: Added allocation accounting with 28 lines of new infrastructure
for tracking allocator state and memory usage patterns across the
filesystem implementation.

ffs-core: Extended core library exports with improved module visibility
for cross-crate access to allocation primitives.

beads: Updated issue tracking database with current project state.

Note: artifacts/ directory excluded from this commit as it contains
ephemeral e2e test outputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Deliverables:
- Add sparse fixtures for:
  - superblock with non-empty sys_chunk_array
  - at least 1 btrfs leaf node block (nodesize) with a few items
- Add harness helpers to parse and validate:
  - mapping of root/chunk_root logical addresses
  - leaf item table bounds

Acceptance:
- btrfs mapping + node parse tasks have fixtures that fail if bounds checks regress.
- Parity counts updated when coverage increases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:38.058404262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:21:04.997119020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:21:04.997097259Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","harness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yi","depends_on_id":"bd-1fo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:05.881201973Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2yi","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:05.800109863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zg","title":"Harness: Add ext4 fixtures for group descriptors + inode parsing","description":"Goal: extend conformance/fixtures beyond superblock so new ext4 parsing work is fixture-backed.

Deliverables:
- Add sparse fixtures for:
  - at least 1 group descriptor (32-byte) and 1 group descriptor (64-byte)
  - at least 1 inode (with extents) and 1 directory inode
  - at least 1 directory block (dir_entry_2)
- Add harness validation helpers that parse these fixtures and assert key fields.

Acceptance:
- New ext4 parsing tasks (group desc, inode parse, dirent parse) have at least one fixture each.
- FEATURE_PARITY and ParityReport are updated in the same change when coverage increases.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:32.375616672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:58:21.396958654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:58:21.396936793Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","harness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zg","depends_on_id":"bd-2dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:05.715953282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2zg","depends_on_id":"bd-3qq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:05.634893223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-375","title":"EPIC: btrfs Read Path & FUSE Mount","description":"# EPIC: btrfs Read Path & FUSE Mount

## PURPOSE
Enable read-only btrfs FUSE mounting, achieving parity with ext4 read-only support. Currently btrfs metadata parsing is at 35% (7/20).

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md, btrfs gaps:
- btrfs transaction parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- btrfs delayed refs parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- btrfs scrub parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- FUSE mount for btrfs: NOT WORKING

However, significant foundation exists:
- Superblock decode: ✅
- B-tree header decode: ✅
- Leaf item metadata decode: ✅
- Internal node parsing: ✅
- sys_chunk mapping: ✅
- Read-only tree walk: ✅
- BtrfsContext in ffs-core: ✅

The remaining gap is wiring tree walking and extent resolution into `FsOps` and then into the FUSE surface.

## CURRENT STATE
- `ffs-ondisk` has comprehensive btrfs parsing
- `ffs-btrfs` provides `walk_tree`
- `ffs-core` has a `BtrfsContext` stub
- `ffs-fuse` operations currently route to ext4-only `FsOps`

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. Implement `BtrfsFsOps` (like `Ext4FsOps` but for btrfs)
2. Wire `ffs-cli mount` path to btrfs ops
3. Implement btrfs inode -> extent resolution sufficient for read-only file reads
4. Add E2E coverage for btrfs mount

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount btrfs.img /mnt` works read-only
2. Basic file operations work: `ls`, `cat`, `stat`
3. Subvolume visibility (at least default subvol)
4. Performance baseline established (hyperfine) for inspect + mount smoke
5. btrfs metadata parsing reaches 60%+ parity

## DEPENDENCIES
- EPIC: Conformance & Quality Infrastructure (bd-2jk) - need fixtures/goldens
- btrfs golden fixtures (bd-2jk.2)

## EXPLICIT V1 EXCLUSIONS (per PLAN_TO_PORT_FRANKENFS_TO_RUST.md)
- Multi-device / RAID: OUT OF SCOPE
- Compression: OUT OF SCOPE
- Send/receive: OUT OF SCOPE
- Write support: OUT OF SCOPE

## RELATED SPEC SECTIONS
- EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md §4-6 (btrfs behavior)
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §2 (btrfs format)

## Success Criteria
1. `ffs inspect <btrfs.img>` and `ffs mount <btrfs.img>` work on the golden fixtures without panics.
2. `scripts/e2e/ffs_btrfs_ro_smoke.sh` passes (or skips cleanly when FUSE unavailable) and produces actionable logs.
3. Unit tests cover:
   - inode lookup by key
   - directory enumeration ordering/offsets
   - extent resolution for file reads","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:56:58.365480057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:02:52.819803453Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","fuse"]}
-{"id":"bd-375.1","title":"Implement BtrfsFsOps for FUSE operations","description":"# Implement BtrfsFsOps for FUSE operations

## GOAL
Add **read-only** btrfs support behind the existing `ffs-core::FsOps` trait so FUSE can mount btrfs images (bd-375.2).

## CURRENT REALITY (CODE)
- `ffs-core` already parses btrfs superblock + sys_chunk mapping at open time (`BtrfsContext`).
- `ffs-core::OpenFs` already has tree-walk helpers (`walk_btrfs_tree`, `walk_btrfs_root_tree`).
- `ffs-btrfs` can walk trees and yields raw leaf entries (`BtrfsLeafEntry { key, data }`).
- What’s missing is decoding leaf payloads into the minimum set of item types needed for VFS operations.

## SCOPE
Implement the minimal btrfs read-only VFS surface:
- `getattr`: inode -> `InodeAttr`
- `lookup`: (parent ino, name) -> child inode
- `readdir`: enumerate dir entries with stable offsets
- `read`: read file bytes via extent resolution

## ARCHITECTURE CHOICE
Prefer **dispatch inside `impl FsOps for OpenFs`** based on `self.flavor`:
- ext4 path: keep existing behavior
- btrfs path: add new btrfs implementations

Alternative (acceptable): introduce a separate `BtrfsFsOps` struct and use it from CLI mount.

## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN
1. Define/implement decoding helpers for the needed btrfs item payloads.
   - Where: `crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs` (higher-level) and/or `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` (pure parsing helpers)
   - Must cover at least:
     - ROOT_ITEM (to find the FS tree root)
     - INODE_ITEM
     - DIR_ITEM / DIR_INDEX
     - EXTENT_DATA (enough to support reading regular files)
2. Add search helpers that operate over a leaf-entry stream:
   - find first matching key
   - iterate a key prefix/range (for directory enumeration)
3. Implement btrfs `FsOps` methods:
   - `getattr`: lookup inode item and map to `InodeAttr`
   - `lookup`: find dir item for `name`, return child ino attr
   - `readdir`: enumerate dir items; offsets must be consistent across calls
   - `read`: resolve file offset to physical via extent mapping + device reads

## FILES TO MODIFY
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` (btrfs path inside `impl FsOps for OpenFs`, plus helper methods)
- `crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs` and/or `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` (item decoding)
- `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` and new targeted harness tests for item decoding using fixtures from bd-2jk.2

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit tests for each decoded item type (parse known bytes -> expected fields).
2. Harness tests that use committed sparse fixtures to validate:
   - inode lookup
   - directory enumeration ordering
   - extent resolution invariants (at least one file read path)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. btrfs `FsOps` paths compile and are reachable (no stub/bail).
2. `getattr/lookup/readdir/read` work for the btrfs reference image used by bd-375.3.
3. Errors for unsupported btrfs features are explicit and actionable.
4. All new behavior is covered by tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:30.943924807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:02:53.096266748Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","harness","ondisk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-375.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:48.566411670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-375.1","depends_on_id":"bd-375","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:30.943924807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

4.  {"id":"bd-375.2","title":"Wire btrfs mount command in ffs-cli","description":"# Wire btrfs mount command in ffs-cli

## GOAL
Enable `ffs mount <btrfs.img> <mountpoint>` to work read-only, parallel to ext4 mount support.

## CURRENT REALITY (CODE)
- CLI entrypoint is `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`.
- `ffs mount` currently opens the image via `OpenFs::open(&cx, image_path)`.
- btrfs mount is explicitly blocked today with a `bail!("btrfs mount not yet supported")`.

## SCOPE
1. Remove the explicit btrfs bail in `mount_cmd()` once bd-375.1 provides btrfs `FsOps` support.
2. Ensure mount options remain explicitly read-only.
3. Improve diagnostics:
   - print btrfs geometry (sectorsize/nodesize) at mount start
   - return clear errors for unsupported btrfs features

## FILES TO MODIFY
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs` (mount command)
- potentially `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` if FUSE wiring needs btrfs-specific behavior (prefer keeping FUSE generic)

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Add an E2E script (bd-375.3) that mounts a generated btrfs image and validates:
   - `ls` / `stat`
   - bounded `find`
   - `cat` of a known file when the generation recipe creates one
2. Add/extend harness tests (from bd-375.1) that validate btrfs `FsOps` correctness without mounting.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `cargo run -p ffs-cli -- mount <btrfs.img> <mnt>` succeeds for the reference image.
2. Clean unmount via `fusermount3 -u` (or `fusermount -u`).
3. Errors are actionable (explicit unsupported-feature messages, not generic panics).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:43.818041537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:02:53.451316213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","cli","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-375.2","depends_on_id":"bd-375","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:43.818041537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-375.2","depends_on_id":"bd-375.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:48.650751368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-375.3","title":"Add E2E smoke tests for btrfs RO mount (FUSE)","description":"# Add E2E smoke tests for btrfs RO mount (FUSE)

## GOAL
Add an end-to-end smoke script that mounts a btrfs reference image read-only and validates basic filesystem operations through FUSE.

## CONTEXT
Unit tests validate parsing and tree walking, but btrfs mount correctness is ultimately a black-box property:
- does `ffs mount` succeed?
- does `ls`/`stat`/`cat` work?
- are subvolumes visible at least for the default subvolume?

This bead adds a reproducible, logged script that answers those questions.

## DELIVERABLES
1. `scripts/e2e/ffs_btrfs_ro_smoke.sh`
2. Uses `scripts/e2e/lib.sh` from bd-2jk.6 for logging/cleanup conventions.
3. Updates `scripts/e2e/README.md` with btrfs-specific notes.

## FIXTURE INPUT
Prefer to generate the btrfs image at runtime (not checked in) using the recipe produced by bd-2jk.2:
- `scripts/fixtures/make_btrfs_reference_image.sh` should create a single-device btrfs image under a temp dir.
- If the environment lacks `mkfs.btrfs` (btrfs-progs), the script must SKIP with a clear message and exit 0.

## SCRIPT REQUIREMENTS
- Same logging + cleanup invariants as bd-2jk.6.
- Must mount to a temp dir and unmount reliably.
- If `/dev/fuse` is not available/unusable: SKIP with a clear message and exit 0.
- Must print detected btrfs geometry (sectorsize/nodesize) from `ffs inspect` into the log header.

## TEST MATRIX
1. `cargo run -p ffs-cli -- inspect <tmp>/e2e.btrfs --json`
2. `cargo run -p ffs-cli -- mount <tmp>/e2e.btrfs <mnt>`
3. Validate (bounded):
   - `ls -la <mnt>`
   - `stat <mnt>`
   - bounded `find` of a small depth
   - `cat` at least one known fixture file *if the generation recipe creates one*
4. Unmount.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Script passes on a correct implementation and fails on obvious regressions (mount failure, empty listing, unreadable known file when expected).
2. Failure output is actionable (command + logs + mount cleanup).
3. Runtime <5 minutes.

## NOTES
- This bead should not add new btrfs functionality; it only validates bd-375.2 end-to-end.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:56:25.606708296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:02:53.096536353Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fuse","harness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-375.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:56:25.606708296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-375.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:56:25.606708296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-375.3","depends_on_id":"bd-375","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:56:25.606708296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-375.3","depends_on_id":"bd-375.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:56:25.606708296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-37g","title":"Perf: Maintain scripts/benchmark.sh + add baselines for key paths","description":"Goal: keep a repeatable benchmark entry point as the CLI/harness evolves.

Deliverables:
- Update scripts/benchmark.sh when commands or binaries change.
- Add baselines for:
  - ffs-cli inspect <fixture>
  - ffs-core open/validate ext4
  - btrfs sys_chunk mapping (once implemented)

Acceptance:
- scripts/benchmark.sh runs successfully on a fresh checkout.
- Baselines are stable enough to detect regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PinkCreek","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:37.930757625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:58:21.589581022Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:58:21.589552609Z","close_reason":"ondisk_parse benchmarks + CLI baselines + baseline-20260211","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37g","depends_on_id":"bd-3q4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:10.672820552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
